title: AFLEWO NAIROBI
description: AFLEWO Nairobi is the inaugural event of all AFLEWO chapters and was first held in 2004 and has birthed the rest of the chapters. It is simply referred as ‘AFLEWO’.The events have been held at CITAM Karen (formerly known as NPC Karen) since 2004 except in 2007 where it was held at Nyayo Stadium. From 2013, the event is now held at Winners’ Chapel, the largest indoor facility in East Africa.Typically held in September unless otherwise, AFLEWO Nairobi has experienced tremendous growth and is now the largest worship event in Kenya bringing together a crowd of more than 15,000 people at once.

title: AFLEWO NAKURU
description: AFLEWO Nakuru was birthed in 2013. The team was part of the 1,000 voice choir drawn from all chapters at the AFLEWO 2013 national event.Just like all AFLEWO chapters, the choir is drawn from different denominations.The team held their first event in Nakuru in August 2014 at Deliverance Church, Nakuru.The event was attended by more than 2,000 people. The chapter continues to grow.

title: AFLEWO MOMBASA
description: AFLEWO Mombasa was first held in 2009 at the beautiful Elim Sanctuary in Makupa. The first event was hosted by AFLEWO Nairobi team assisted with the Mombasa team.AFLEWO Mombasa has been credited with uniting the church in Mombasa by bringing together a choir from different denominations.The event is typically held in September at the Elim Church and is attended by more than 5,000 every year.

title: AFLEWO NYERI
description: AFLEWO Nyeri was founded in 2010 and the first event was held at PCEA Nyamachaki in October 2010.The event has steadily grown and is attended by over 2,000 people from the town of Nyeri.The event is supported by the pastors’ fellowship in Nyeri.

title: AFLEWO TANZANIA
description: AFLEWO Tanzania was founded in 2010 and held its first event in April 2011 at Aga Khan Diamond Hall in Dar-es-Salaam.The event was a huge success drawing a crowd of more than 4,000 people.The event has continued to grow and has held 3 more events at CCC Upanga church.Also known as AFLEWO Dar, AFLEWO Tanzania was the first international event for AFLEWO.

title: AFLEWO Rwanda
description: AFLEWO Rwanda also known as AFLEWO Kigali was founded in 2011 and was first held at CLA, Nyarutarama in November 2011.It brought together a choir from different denominations in Kigali.AFLEWO Kigali held its second event in March 2014 as part of the country’s 20 year commemoration of the genocide. The theme was healing and reconciliation.

title: AFLEWO MERU
description: AFLEWO Meru was founded in 2012 and held its first event at Gikumene High School on 20th April, 2012.AFLEWO Meru choir was also part of the AFLEWO 2013 1000 voice choir held at Winners’ Chapel on the eve of Kenya’s Independence Day to commemorate 50 years of independence.The chapter continues to grow and held its second event in September, 2014 at KEMU Chapel.

title: with the vision to bring hope in Jesus to Africa. As Sing Africa members have graduated from Daystar University

type: The AFLEWO Story and the event.
AFLEWO (an acronym for AFRICA LET’S WORSHIP) is an annual all night musical worship event which was started in Nairobi in 2004

The Event is led by a strong talented music team comprising of singers and instrumentalists drawn from different Christian denominations and churches spread across Nairobi and its environs. The music team leads the congregation through a night of gospel praise and worship.

title: together with an able team of logistics personnel plan work hand in hand to stage an annual world class event!

type: The AFLEWO Event aims at involving the entire Christian community in any given AFLEWO town in a night of Christian worship and thus our participants have typically included young
title: middle-aged and senior-adults.

type: Introducing Individual Commitment To A Group Effort .

Teamwork is the ability to work together toward a common vision. The ability to direct individual accomplishments toward organizational objectives. It is the fuel that allows common people to attain uncommon results!

The AFLEWO management also referred to as the as the AFLEWO Summit is comprised of individuals drawn from different churches each sharing a common vision for prayer and worship in not only Kenya but also AFRICA. Thus the AFLEWO Management comprises of the following offices.
The AFLEWO team is given spiritual guidance by a team of dedicated pastors. Since AFLEWO draws an inter-denominational team
title: the pastors are also drawn from various churches.

AFLEWO (Africa Let’s Worship) is an interdenominational Christian movement that has spent over 20 years building a "prophetic house" over Africa. It is defined by its massive
title: all-night worship events and a multi-national network of alumni and volunteers.

type: The movement was birthed from the alumni of Sing Africa
title: a ministry of Daystar University Christian Fellowship Ministries (established in 1996).

type: The Vision (Oct 2003): Members from different generations of Sing Africa sought to create a united front for the church to speak hope into Africa
title: which was then often labeled the "dark continent".

type: The Name: The phrase "Africa Let's Worship" was suggested by a leader named Ruguru
title: and the acronym AFLEWO was adopted.

type: Founders & Visionaries: Key founding leadership includes Timothy Kaberia.
2004 (Inaugural): Held at CITAM Karen (formerly NPC Karen) in Nairobi.
2007 (Stadium Event): The only Nairobi event held at Nyayo Stadium to accommodate growing crowds.
2013–Present: Moved to Winners' Chapel International on Likoni Road
title: Nairobi
description: currently the largest indoor facility in East Africa.

title: 000-voice choir drawn from all chapters to celebrate Kenya's 50th Independence anniversary.

type: 2024: Celebrated its 20th Anniversary ("AFLEWO at 20") on September 20 with the theme "Heirs of God’s Glory".
2025: Most recent flagship event held on October 3
AFLEWO has established 8+ chapters across Africa to foster local unity:
Nyeri (2010): Supported by local pastors' fellowships; first held at PCEA Nyamachaki.
Meru (2012): First held at Gikumene High School.
AFLEWO Tanzania (Dar es Salaam): Founded 2010; first international chapter.
AFLEWO Rwanda (Kigali): Founded 2011; notable 2014 event focused on genocide commemoration.
Leadership Team: Currently led by Hubert de Rogue Maura (Chairman/Team Leader
title: AFLEWO Nairobi).

type: Pastoral Advisory Board: A board of pastors from various denominations provides spiritual oversight.
Expansion Department: A dedicated wing that scouts for new locations and "envisions" local pastors before setting up new chapters.
Participation: The choir is the core of the event. Auditions and registration are required (typically free).

title: Co-operative Bank of Kenya as a major 2025 sponsor) and community contributions via Paybill numbers.

type: Music Rights: AFLEWO does not produce commercial albums; they use songs in the public domain or by various artists. Commercial recordings are handled by the parent ministry
title: Sing Africa.

type: WhatsApp: Recently launched an official channel for real-time updates.
Archives: Video history and founder stories are available on their YouTube Channel.
